steps.title: Find the minimum of f(x) using the vertex formula
steps.content:
For $f(x) = 4x^2 + 64x + 262$, identify $a = 4$ and $b = 64$.

Substitute into the vertex formula:
$$x = -\frac{64}{2(4)} = -\frac{64}{8} = -8$$
The minimum of $f(x)$ occurs at $x = -8$.

steps.title: Apply the transformation g(x) = f(x+5)
steps.content:
Since $g(x) = f(x+5)$, the function $g$ reaches its minimum when its input equals the x-value where $f$ is minimized.

Set the argument equal to $-8$:
$$x + 5 = -8$$
Solve for $x$:
$$x = -13$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
